questions.text: Integration is the inverse process of:
questions.explanation: Integration is the inverse process of differentiation. It is also called anti-derivative.
This is the power rule of integration: ∫x^n dx = x^(n+1)/(n+1) + C, valid for n ≠ -1

questions.text: What is the constant of integration?
questions.correctAnswer: The arbitrary constant C added to the result of indefinite integration is called the constant of integration.
questions.explanation: Since derivative of a constant is zero, indefinite integrals include an arbitrary constant C to represent the family of antiderivatives.
